titleStmt.title: Note from Sir Wilfrid Spender, 28 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Bliss Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A note from Sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender, most probably to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). In this note, Spender writes that he has mistaken the time and has no time to write a full letter; he asks the recipient of the note to destroy all of his letters apart from those which criticise the publication of his letters. This is presumably related to previous incidents where numerous of Spender's letters were published in the Press without his permission, subsequently causing him trouble with his commanding officer.Sir Wilfrid Spender was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender wrote a collection of letters to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Aug 28 Mistake time
no time to write
you tear up any & all letters
except those dealing with the incident
of publication of my letters to show that
it was done entirely against my will.
